blocks.content: Thursday morning. Hallway near the gym. Daniel came through the way he always did — like the hall was his and everybody else was just borrowing space.
He walked right through Jonathan's shoulder. Didn't stop. Didn't look back.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Better watch where you walking. I'll catch you when you ain't got your boys around you.
Jonathan stopped. Didn't spin around, didn't rush it. He just stopped walking and turned around slow.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh — do you know who you talking to right now? Like, genuinely? Because from where I'm standing, you just walked up to the wrong one and tried to make it seem like you had a point. You don't have a point. You just looking for something that ain't yours to find.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Look at you. Can't even walk through a hallway without the whole team around you. That's what you are, man. You can't run a play, you can't catch a ball, you can't do nothing by yourself. Everything you got came from other people setting it up for you. You ride a machine. Pull the machine away, and you nothing. Everybody sees it. I'm just the first one saying it.
I watched Jonathan's jaw. Something shifted — not much, but I saw it. Daniel had found something that wasn't completely nothing.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Okay. I'm going to give you one chance to hear yourself right now before this goes somewhere you don't want it to go. Everything I got, I put in. Every route, every rep, every early morning and every late night — I earned that. You don't know what I've put in, and you don't know what I'm capable of. So I really need you to think about what you're about to say next.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: See, that's the thing. You think being sensitive about it means it ain't true. Everybody on this team knows what the real deal is. You got talent — okay, fine. But you got no edge. Somebody actually gets in your face for real, pushes the right buttons, and you go quiet. I've seen it. And I'm not the only one. Matter of fact, Crystal was talking about you the other day. Wasn't saying much nice either. Just thought you should know how that's landing out here.
There it was.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: BRUH. What did you just say.
Not a question. A line.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: You heard me. I'm just telling you what's out there. You can be mad about it, but that's on you, not me.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Nah, nah — you need to tell me right now what you think you know, because you just put her name in your mouth and I'm not doing that with you. You don't get to come at me with that. You don't get to come at her with that. So say it again. Say what you said again and look me in the eye when you do it. Because I need you to understand something very clearly — I swear on God, I will drop you in this hallway. Right now. I don't care who sees it, I don't care what happens after. You keep going, I will make sure you remember this conversation for the rest of your life.
He wasn't loud. That was what got me. He was heated — genuinely — but his voice had gone down, not up. Like the temperature in him had gone somewhere past the part that shouts.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: There it is. See, all I had to do was push a little and—
That's when Marcus and DeShawn came around the corner.
They didn't say a word. They just came and stood behind Jonathan — two linemen, both of them bigger than Daniel by a full size. Unhurried. Like they had nowhere else to be.
Daniel's eyes went to them. His whole calculation shifted in about half a second.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Pshhhh.
He smirked. Like this was beneath him now. Like he'd decided to be done with it on his own terms.
He walked. Still performing — the walk had a whole story in it — but he walked.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Mm.
He didn't say anything else. He just watched Daniel go down the hall, and then he turned back around like he'd just dealt with a notification and put the phone down.
